summary: Add the C11 MCP tool contract foundation: the ten normative tool names, strict project-relative request validation, mutating-tool classification, and deny-by-default permission state transitions.

decisions.decision: Validate all tool names and project-relative path arguments in an application-owned contract module; classify write_file and set_parameters as mutating and deny them by default while read-only tools begin session-allowed.
decisions.rationale: Appendix B requires exact tool names and project-relative paths, while FR-11.6 requires per-tool permission gates with safe defaults before a stdio server can dispatch commands.

testEvidence.criterion: MCP contract tests fail before the new module and pass for read validation, traversal rejection, mutating payload validation, format validation, and permission transitions.
testEvidence.test: Run the focused MCP contract suite once after implementation.

red.outcome: RED: the MCP contract module was not yet present before this unit began.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 3 passed (3); Duration 405ms.

testEvidence.criterion: The MCP foundation remains type-safe and source-policy clean.
testEvidence.test: Run TypeScript and repository lint/source policy once after implementation.

red.outcome: RED: the MCP contract module was not yet present before this unit began.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false; pnpm.cmd lint
green.outcome: GREEN: TypeScript exited 0; Checked 433 files in 222ms with source policy passed.

questions: The stdio MCP server, Appendix B response handlers, command-bus dispatch, diff-review integration, and process-inspection AC-11.c remain pending; this is contract groundwork only.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
